title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 28 - 2017: Roman fucking Timeline bitches

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog (4:23 AM) recapping April 28th, which he spent almost entirely coding and designing an HTML Roman history timeline website. He briefly visited his mom to help with her back, then returned to work on linking king entries between horizontal and vertical views, troubleshooting table layout issues in Dreamweaver, and planning color backgrounds by dynasty era. He mentions getting high while working and finding it enhances his engagement with Roman history.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: excited and optimistic; believes it will look really cool when finished, estimates another week or two

stances.topic: getting high while working on the timeline
stances.position: enjoys it and plans to do more; finds it fun and makes looking at the project feel rewarding
feels he has been learning a lot and experiences quantum jumps in knowledge when viewing it from new angles

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-LOG for April 28th. It's 4.23 in the morning on April 29th right now.
I was working on my timeline all day. I did go to my mom's house to help vacuum the house and help work some things in the morning, move stuff around.
So, I then came back here and was working on my Rome timeline, cutting and pasting, like, all of the years, you know, filling in the years, and I think dragging stuff around.
And then, towards the end of the night, I was doing, um, I decided to, um, I didn't like how the kings were kind of blended in everything, so I wanted to have their own background as white.
Or, and then I changed my mind, have their background on the vertical column the same as their background on the horizontal one.
Because then, after that, I decided that now's the time to link it.
So, yeah, whenever you hit the king on the horizontal or the vertical, it takes you to the other one. It's perfect.

So, I'm really looking forward to finishing this thing. Um, this page, um, I'm glad that I'm, you know, I separated one part of my timeline just to finish, and you know, know what it looks like when it's done.
Um, I think I could finish this one in, like, another week or something.
But, time's not really of the essence anymore. I got a good tax return.
And, uh, anyway.
Um, yeah. Um, another week or two.
Uh, right now, it doesn't really look that good because the columns are, like, warped. I don't know why.
Anyway, so, uh, whenever that happens, you just rebuild it, you know, just to find out what part is screwed up.
But when it's on Dreamweaver, it looks fine.
Um, so I think tomorrow I'll just, uh, well, um, I'll, uh, um, I'll condense the words down.
And, um, also, um, I also know this one PHP script to make it so you can do the mouse over.
But I might want to hold, I think I want to hold off on that.
Um, but, uh, yeah, um, colorize the backgrounds and link them.
But on my Rome page, I made it so that the kings are centered with the date below them.
And, uh, it took me a long time to do that.
And I don't think, I wish I didn't do that because it took me a long time.
I don't want to do it with the rest of it.
Um, but I will put the white backgrounds for the rest of the kings for the rest of the timeline.
And then just drag it over and, you know, leave it looking the rest, like, ordered the way the others on the left with the date on the left.
But, um, but the white background really does help.
Um, and then have it inside of a table representing the, like, the era or the dynasty.
Um, um, but you, but if I wanted to, because the king has their own table, I could view the background of that too.
But I don't know, we'll see.
Probably nobody knows what I'm talking about.
But it'll look really cool when it's done.
And I think people will know what I'm talking about.
Um, so that's all I did today.
I listened to, uh, well, because I was copying and pasting, I was, you know, kind of listening to art, reading what I was doing.
So I wasn't really listening that much.
But, um, I was listening to, uh, Strabo.
Um, talking about the, um, geography.
Um, I was having an asthma all day, though.
I'm really high right now.
But I was having an asthma all day.
Um, when I had to scrape it that, like, two, three days ago, I had never got better from that.
So I had to be careful.
Um, but I think I'm going to get more.
Because being high and working on this timeline is really fun.
It's really cool to look at it high.
Um, and wow, yeah, I've been learning a lot about Rome, Roman history.
And then the last, just the last couple days on the timeline.
It's cool when you have these, like, quantum jumps in, uh, knowledge.
Because you're looking at something, like, a new way, you know.
Like, with my timeline, all of a sudden I'm looking at it that way.
As opposed to just listening to a book.
